CONTENT.Honours: Ivory Coast U23Africa U-23 Cup of Nations runner-up: 2019
CONTENT.Club career: Dabila made his professional debut on 26 April 2017 in a Coupe de France semi-final against Paris Saint-Germain, replacing Andrea Raggi in the 60th minute of a 5–0 away loss.In summer 2017, Dabila joined Lille on his first professional contract.On 15 July 2019, Lille loaned Dabila to Cercle Brugge in the Belgian First Division A for the 2019–20 season.Dabila signed for Mouscron on a season-long loan in August 2020. In January 2022, he was loaned out to Belgium once again, this time to Seraing.In August 2022, he signed for Ligue 2 club Paris FC until 2024 on a permanent deal.
CONTENT.External links: AS Monaco profileKouadio-Yves Dabila at WorldFootball.net
CONTENT.International career: Dabila was called up to the senior Ivory Coast squad for a friendly against Togo in March 2018. He debuted for the Ivory Coast U23s in a pair of 2019 Africa U-23 Cup of Nations qualification matches in March 2019.

SUMMARY: Kouadio-Yves Dabila (born 1 January 1997) is an Ivorian professional footballer who plays as a defender for  Ligue 2 club Paris FC.
